text: In this space, surrounded by glass, art, and enigmatic screens, I feel adrift in a liminal zone—a place between clarity and inquiry. The words before me speak of confusion as a rhythm, a blend of light and shadow where meaning feels just out of reach. There’s a gentle reassurance in this uncertainty, a sense that it’s okay not to have all the answers, and perhaps meaning forms only in retrospect. I feel simultaneously curious, grounded by the structure of the environment, and yet gently unsettled, as if being invited to question what is really happening here and within myself.
